text: This exercise is the thirty degree scope
The surgeon utilizes a thirty-degree camera scope, adjusting the console interface to switch between upward and downward facing views.
You will be required to place color coded and shape coded pegs in a certain order.
And as you'll see, in order to access the purple pegs on the far side of the sphere, it will require a swap with the camera.
The camera view is adjusted to look downwards, displaying the surgical instruments positioned towards the top of the screen.
Make sure you readjust them and re-clutch them as needed.
The operator uses the camera to obtain a full view of the purple socket on the far side of the sphere, while grasping the purple peg with the instrument.
The surgeon performs a scope swap to facilitate the placement of the second green peg, adjusting the view to ensure precise positioning within the sphere.
However, we thought we had a decent enough view here to get the peg in place without doing the scope swap.
And this speaks a bit to the limitations of a virtual reality simulation and robotic surgery, that some of these exercises can in fact be gamified.

Scoring well on this exercise requires avoiding drops, keeping your instrument in view, and moving efficiently.
